verdict_reasoning: B2M (beta-2-microglobulin) is the non-covalently associated light chain of MHC class I complexes. It lacks a transmembrane domain of its own but is a constitutive, stable non-covalent partner of the MHC class I heavy chain (HLA-A/B/C), which is a classical single-pass TM protein. The B2M–heavy-chain complex is assembled in the ER and co-trafficked to the plasma membrane where it is stably displayed on the outer leaflet of nearly all nucleated cells. This is the canonical, baseline surface state of B2M — not a transient or condition-dependent event. The NCBI summary explicitly states it is 'found in association with the MHC class I heavy chain on the surface of nearly all nucleated cells.' This fits the `stable_complex_partner` mechanism: B2M reaches the outer leaflet as an integral member of a co-trafficked TM-anchored complex. It is a well-established target of surface-directed therapeutics and CAR-T programs that engage MHC-I on the cell surface.
